title: Iliad, Book 1: The Wrath of Achilles
description: Book 1 of Homer's Iliad: The Wrath of Achilles.
introduction: Book 1 of the Iliad: The Wrath of Achilles. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.original_text: Μῆνιν ἄειδε, θεά, Πηληϊάδεω Ἀχιλῆος οὐλομένην, ἣ μυρί᾿ Ἀχαιοῖς ἄλγε᾿ ἔθηκε, πολλὰς δ᾿ ἰφθίμους ψυχὰς Ἄϊδι προΐαψεν ἡρώων.
translation.text: Sing, O goddess, the wrath of Achilles, son of Peleus, the accursed wrath that brought countless sorrows upon the Achaeans, and hurled many mighty souls of heroes down to Hades, making them prey for dogs and all the birds — and the will of Zeus was accomplished. Begin from the time when first Agamemnon, king of men, and godlike Achilles quarreled and parted. Apollo, angered at the king, sent a plague upon the army.
